steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content:
Before the blank: ...veins that provide structural support — a complete independent clause.
After the blank: Decreasing in thickness from the center to the edges, these veins allow... — another complete independent clause with an introductory participial phrase.

Two independent clauses must be separated by terminal punctuation (or a semicolon).

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Choice C uses a period: support. Decreasing — correctly ending the first sentence and starting a new one whose introductory Decreasing...edges phrase modifies these veins.

A, B, and D create comma splices or run-ons.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
